{{Infobox character
| name = Leah
| appears_in = The Traveler's Gate Trilogy
| titles = Highness, Heiress Leah, Heiress of the Damascan royal family, Successor to the throne
| abilities = take a life with a stray thought, scrying lens, construct crystals from the same stone as her bracelet, tap into her Source, reach into Lirial and summon crystal, Ragnarus Traveler
| affiliations = Damascan royal family
| introduced = tg-01-house-of-blades
}}

''See also: [[Leah Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Leah is a woman of dual identities who transitioned from living as a simple villager to occupying a position of significant authority. Originally from [[Bel Calem]], she moved to a Myrian village two years prior to live with her aunt, [[Nurita]], following the death of her mother, [[Kelia]]. During her time in the village, she was known for her natural charm and ability to connect with the local people. She is described as having dark hair, blue eyes, and a silver bracelet featuring a white crystal that she wears constantly as a memento of her mother. As her role evolved, Leah took on much greater responsibilities, eventually appearing in regal attire such as gold, black, crimson, or red dresses, often accompanied by a crown or silver circlet. In her capacity as a leader, she manages petitions and issues orders to officers, messengers, and [[Travelers]]. Her transformation is marked by a commanding presence and physical changes, including the appearance of a red eye and a raven on her shoulder, which lends her an [[aura]] similar to a powerful territorial queen. Despite her high status and the burdens of her office, she maintains connections to her past, including a protective, sisterly bond with [[Rutha]].}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Leah, the daughter of [[Kelia]] and [[Zakareth the Sixth]], is a blue-eyed girl from [[Bel Calem]] who moved to [[Myria]] two years ago. Identified as Highness, she wears a silver bracelet with a dangling white crystal and possesses the terrifying ability to end a [[life]] with a mere thought. After being taken by enemy soldiers, she was held as a captive in a collar and sack before being moved to the [[Overlord]]'s house by the [[Overlord]] himself. While she was scheduled to be sacrificed to [[Zakareth]]’s [[Territory]] and held by [[Malachi]], Leah acted as a spy who used faked tears to manipulate men. She eventually traveled with [[Simon]] and [[Alin]], as [[Simon]] sought to use the power of a [[Traveler]] to ensure her safety.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] is the daughter of [[Kelia]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2">''House of Blades'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] is a girl in [[Myria]] who moved from [[Bel Calem]] two years prior.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] wears a silver bracelet with a clear white crystal dangling from the chain.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] has blue eyes.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] is a character with bright blue eyes who carries a bloodstained [[sword]] and a crystal bracelet.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] is referred to as Highness.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] has the ability to take a [[life]] with a stray thought.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] was a captive in chains and a collar.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4">''House of Blades'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] is present in the cave.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] was taken by enemy soldiers.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c5">''House of Blades'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] is a person [[Simon]] remembers wearing a [[steel]] collar and a shapeless brown sack.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c6">''House of Blades'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] is someone [[Simon]] wishes to save.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c7">''House of Blades'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] is someone [[Alin]] thinks about.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c8">''House of Blades'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] wants to free [[Leah]] with the power of a [[Traveler]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9">''House of Blades'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Nurita]] is [[Leah]]'s aunt.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c15">''House of Blades'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] is a person scheduled to be sacrificed to [[Zakareth]]’s [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] is a captive held by [[Malachi]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16">''House of Blades'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] is the sister of [[Nurita]]'s niece.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17">''House of Blades'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] was taken by the [[Overlord]] to the [[Overlord]]'s house.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] is searching for [[Leah]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c19">''House of Blades'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] is a girl who may be at the house.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20">''House of Blades'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] is a spy.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c21">''House of Blades'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] is a character who fakes shock and tears to manipulate men.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] sounds uncomfortably like her aunt when she takes the lead.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] is traveling with [[Simon]] and [[Alin]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c22">''House of Blades'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] wants [[Leah]] to get to safety.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]] is the daughter of [[Zakareth the Sixth]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c23">''House of Blades'', chapter 23</ref>}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The daughter of [[King]] [[Zakareth]] and [[Kelia]], Leah is a [[Ragnarus Traveler]] and the second in line for the throne of [[Damasca]]. Having spent most of her [[life]] outside of [[Myria]], she was sent to the region to connect with her mother's family and learn about common villagers. While observing events through a scrying lens, she utilizes her four-pointed crystal star [[Source]] to summon and [[construct]] crystals from the same stone as her milky white bracelet. After opposing [[Talos]] in the succession, she eventually accepted the burden of her office and ascended as the [[Queen of Damasca]]. During a battle, she arrived wearing a [[ruby]] circlet and red-and-gold dress to command the [[Incarnation]] of [[Valinhall]] to await execution.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] uses a scrying lens to observe events.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] wears a crystal bracelet.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is a character who opposed [[Talos]] in the succession.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|In [[Damasca]], [[Leah]] is known as [[Heiress]] [[Leah]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]]'s family holds the throne in [[Damasca]] and has no surname.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] ran into [[Leah]] outside his room.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] lived most of her [[life]] outside of [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is a character who can [[construct]] crystals from the same stone as her bracelet.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] possesses a [[Source]] in the form of a four-pointed crystal star.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah's Source]] is linked to her [[essence]] and allows her to summon and empower [[Lirial]] devices.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is the daughter of [[King]] [[Zakareth]] and [[Talos]]'s sister.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is an [[Heiress]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] wears a long red dress.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] wears a silvered [[steel]] circlet set with a [[ruby]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] wears a milky white crystal on her left wrist.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is an [[Heiress]] and the fourth child of [[King]] [[Zakareth]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is second in line for the throne of [[Damasca]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is an [[Heiress]] of the [[Damascan]] royal family.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is a powerful [[Traveler]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] was sent to [[Myria]] to connect with her mother's family and learn about common villagers.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]]'s mother was from [[Myria]] and her aunt is [[Nurita]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] sat on a wooden bench thinking about things she should have said.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] has a position of great authority in [[Damasca]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is the daughter of [[Kelia]] and the [[Heiress]] of [[Damasca]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is a [[Ragnarus Traveler]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] accepted the burden of her office and swore to fulfill it.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is the [[Successor]] to the throne.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c17">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is in charge at the location.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c19">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] has a history in [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] possesses a crystal bracelet and can tap into her [[Source]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] uses a crystal bracelet to reach into [[Lirial]] and summon crystal.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is searching for her brother.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] arrived at the battle wearing her crown with her [[Lirial Source]] nearly tapped out.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is a character who wears a red-and-gold dress and a [[ruby]] circlet.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] watches [[Simon]] dash off into the distance.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is the person [[Indirial]] refers to as 'Doll'.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] commanded the [[Incarnation]] of [[Valinhall]] to drop his weapons and await execution.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] was safe in [[Enosh]] at the [[Grandmasters’ palace]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is [[Damascan]] royalty wearing a crown of [[Damasca]] and a silk dress.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] had been in the village for almost three years.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is to be served as [[Queen]] by [[Talos]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] used power against the [[Incarnation]] earlier.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is the [[Queen of Damasca]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c25">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] is a [[Ragnarus Traveler]] and a [[Queen of Damasca]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c25" />}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A blue-eyed, dark-haired [[Overlord]] and [[Queen]], Leah holds a [[Traveler]]'s bond with both Ragnarus and the [[Crystal Fields]] of [[Lirial]]. After calling upon [[Simon]] for assistance, she managed her duties by explaining mission delays to seven [[Travelers]] and utilizing floating crystals to collect reports. During the conflict against [[Alin]]'s army, she wielded a tall [[Lightning Spear]] and a [[Ragnarus spear]], though the use of the former left her body shaking with pain. Leah eventually entered the [[Crimson Vault]] by birthright and stood at the edge of the [[Elysian Gate]] behind a wall of golems and [[Travelers]] to face the [[Ragnarus Incarnation]]. Clad in an expensive red dress and a silver circlet, she utilized her father's eye—a glowing red substitute for her left eye—while sealing the [[Incarnation]]'s legs with crystal.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] has called [[Simon]] to help with missions.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c3">''City of Light'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] has dark hair and blue eyes.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] has a birthright to open the [[Crimson Vault]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c4">''City of Light'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] holds a [[Traveler]]'s bond with the [[Crystal Fields]] of [[Lirial]] and Ragnarus.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] is an [[Overlord]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] said she needed [[Simon]]'s help.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6">''City of Light'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] can afford the [[Queen's communications crystal]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c8">''City of Light'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] had to explain her mission's delay to seven [[Travelers]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9">''City of Light'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] wields a tall [[Lightning Spear]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] possesses a scrying lens that is a round crystal mirror.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] is a queen.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11">''City of Light'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] wears a crown with a [[ruby]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] is part of the group taking out [[Alin]]'s army.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] possesses a [[Ragnarus spear]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c12">''City of Light'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]]'s body shakes with pain after using the [[Lightning Spear]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14">''City of Light'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] needs to take care of [[Leah]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c15">''City of Light'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] possesses a token that can send someone directly into safe lands.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] almost got [[Alin]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16">''City of Light'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] is a [[Queen]] and an [[Overlord]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17">''City of Light'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] is currently in [[Avernus]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] has ancestors who used the Hanging [[Trees]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19">''City of Light'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] has a camp outside of [[Cana]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] stepped out of the [[Avernus Gate]] with a raven on each shoulder.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c20">''City of Light'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] is the only active [[Ragnarus Traveler]] left.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]] is probably with [[Leah]] on the hill.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] uses floating [[Lirial]] crystals to collect reports from [[Travelers]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22">''City of Light'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] suggests she can follow the [[King]] into [[the House]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] walked into the [[Crimson Vault]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c24">''City of Light'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]]'s battle plan involves forming lines outside of a Gate and fighting a retreat into [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25">''City of Light'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] can track the target's destination.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] spoke to [[Alin]], stating that they allowed [[Grandmaster Naraka]] to come as a peace offering.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26">''City of Light'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] has replaced her left eye with a glowing red substitute.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] wears a long, expensive dress of pure red and a silver circlet.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] has a raven on her left shoulder.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] knows the [[King]]'s real goal is [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] is standing behind a wall of golems and [[Travelers]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] was at the edge of the [[Elysian Gate]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] sealed the [[Incarnation]]'s legs with a layer of crystal.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] is facing the [[Ragnarus Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] tells [[Leah]] to remember that she should save power for emergencies.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] possesses her father's eye.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|“We met a party of village leaders out on the road, Highness.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|"Leah. What would she say, when she saw him free her with the power of a Traveler?"<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|“The Overlord took her himself. I think they brought her to the Overlord’s house.”<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“And of course you know my daughter, Heiress Leah.”<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|"I accept this burden," Leah said, recovering herself.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate characters]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
[[Category:Travelers]]
